decisions.decision: Broaden the existing matched-account dispatcher branch in `backend/server.py` to recognize `positions` alongside `risk` and `exposure` so `/v1/accounts/{id}/positions` inherits the same matched-path handling instead of introducing a separate route case.
alternatives_rejected: Add a standalone `/v1/accounts/{id}/positions` branch with its own method handling.
Leave `positions` unmatched in the shared account-read branch and let it fall through to a generic 404.

decisions.decision: Preserve the shared GET-only `405 method_not_allowed` guard for all matched `/v1/accounts/{id}/{resource}` reads, then add only an additive `positions` dispatch arm so the existing `/risk` and `/exposure` behavior remains unchanged.
alternatives_rejected: Special-case non-GET `/positions` requests with different error handling.
Refactor the `/risk` and `/exposure` branches while adding `/positions`.

decisions.decision: Keep this child step scoped to backend route plumbing in `backend/server.py`, using the dedicated `get_account_positions(account_id)` getter as the target seam and deferring broader `/positions` regression expansion to the later backend-test phase.
alternatives_rejected: Expand this step into the full `/positions` serializer or HTTP regression rollout.
Dispatch `/positions` through `get_account_exposure(...)` instead of the dedicated getter seam.

steps.task: Inspect the current `/v1/accounts/{id}/...` branch in `route_request(...)` and confirm the `get_account_positions(account_id)` prerequisite seam exists so the dispatcher change can stay additive and avoid altering the current `/risk` and `/exposure` contracts.

Extend the existing matched-account route predicate from `{ "risk", "exposure" }` to `{ "risk", "exposure", "positions" }` while preserving the shared non-GET `405 method_not_allowed` branch for any matched account-read path.
Add a `positions` dispatch arm that routes `GET /v1/accounts/{id}/positions` to `get_account_positions(account_id)` and leaves the current `risk` and `exposure` dispatch paths behaviorally unchanged.

Sanity-check the resulting diff to ensure this child step remains backend-only route plumbing in `backend/server.py`, with no incidental changes to the existing account-read surface beyond recognizing and dispatching `positions` through the shared account branch.
